title: Same-snapshot ranking expert over (Race, Lap) groups
summary: Adjacent ranking literature suggests LambdaMART-style objectives are strong when the real question is relative urgency inside a group.
rationale: Adjacent ranking literature suggests LambdaMART-style objectives are strong when the real question is relative urgency inside a group. Here, pit calls are often competitive within the same race lap: who is most exposed to undercut, warm-up disadvantage, or tyre fade right now.
implementation_hint: Build a second model with query groups = rows sharing (Race_Year, LapNumber). Add peer-relative features such as rank of LapTime_Delta, rank of TyreLife, gap to same-lap median pace, teammate-relative order, and compound rarity within the group. Train LightGBM/XGBoost ranking, then use blocked OOF rank scores as a feature or blend with the pointwise classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering of borderline cases near shared pit windows, especially when absolute thresholds miss relative race context.
risk: Some groups will have few positives or no positives; ranking loss is only indirectly aligned with rowwise ROC AUC and needs clean group-blocked OOF generation.
